lines.en_lit: except for: “You shall not take “You shall not take and any similar to it,

Come hear Rabbi Yehuda says: any from “You shall not take and below, repentance atones. from “You shall not take and above, repentance suspends and Yom Kippur atones. “You shall not take and anything similar to it.

Come hear Since it was stated at Horeb repentance: “Absolve,” might even “You shall not take among them; the verses states: “Will not absolve” might also for those who are liable all other prohibitions; the verse states: “His name.” does not absolve the one His name, but He absolves those who are liable all other prohibitions

This is tanna’im, as it was taught For what does repentance atone? for a positive and for a negative through a positive mitzva. And for what does repentance suspend and Yom Kippur atone? for karet, and for death court, and for full-fledged negative

The Master said: Since it was stated at Horeb “Absolve.” From where do we As it was taught Rabbi Elazar says: It is not possible to say “absolve” “will not absolve” is already stated And it is not possible to say “will not absolve,” “absolve” is already stated. How so? absolves those who repent and does not absolve those who do not repent.

Rabbi Matya ben Ḥarash asked Rabbi Elazar ben Azarya in Rome: Have you heard four distinctions of atonement that Rabbi Yishmael would derive? He said to him: They are three and repentance with each one.

one violates a positive and repents, he is forgiven even before he moves as it is stated: “Return, you backsliding children, one violates a prohibition and repents, repentance suspends and Yom Kippur atones as it is stated: “For on this day shall atonement be made for you, from all your sins” one commits karet or death court and repents, repentance and Yom Kippur suspend and suffering absolves as it is stated: “Then will I visit their transgression with the rod, and their iniquity with strokes”

But one who has desecration of name, his repentance has no power to suspend nor does Yom Kippur to atone nor does suffering to absolve Rather, all these suspend and death absolves as it is stated: “And the Lord of Hosts revealed Himself to my ears: This iniquity shall not be atoned for until you die”

What are the circumstances desecration of name? Rav said: For example, me, if I take meat from a butcher and do not give him money immediately, Abaye said: They taught this only in a place where they do not ask But in a place where they ask we have no with

Ravina said: city Meḥasya is a place where they ask when Abaye bought meat from two partners, he would give the money to this one and the money to this one, And afterward he would bring them together and perform the calculation

Rabbi Yoḥanan said: For example, me, if walk four cubits without Torah and without phylacteries, Yitzḥak from the school of Rabbi Yannai said: Any one’s friends are embarrassed on account of his reputation, this is a desecration of name. Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: for example, when people say May his Master forgive so-and-so

Abaye said: As it was taught “And you shall love the Lord your God” that you shall make the name of Heaven beloved. in that he read and learn and serve Torah scholars, and he should be pleasant with people in his business transactions. What do people say about Fortunate is his father who taught him Torah, fortunate is his teacher who taught him Torah, woe to the people who have not studied Torah. So-and-so, who taught him Torah, see how pleasant are his ways, how proper are his deeds. The verse states about him “You are My servant, Israel, in whom I will be glorified”

But one who reads and learns and serves Torah scholars, but his business practices are not faithfully, and he does not speak pleasantly with other people, what do people say about him? Woe to so-and-so who studied Torah, woe to his father who taught him Torah, woe to his teacher who taught him Torah. So-and-so who studied Torah, see how destructive are his deeds, and how ugly are his ways. About him the verse states “Men said of them: These are the people of the Lord, yet they had to leave His land”
Rabbi Ḥama bar Ḥanina said: Great is repentance, as it brings healing to the world, as it is stated: “I will heal their backsliding, I will love them freely”

Rabbi Ḥama bar Ḥanina raised a contradiction It is written “Return, you backsliding children” initially you were backsliding, But it is written: “I will heal your backsliding” not difficult: Here, out of love; there, out of fear.

Rabbi Yehuda raised a contradiction It is written: “Return, you backsliding children I will heal your backsliding” for I am a lord to you, and I will take you one from a city, and two from a family” not difficult: Here, out of love or fear, there, through suffering, Rabbi Levi said: Great is repentance, as it reaches the heavenly throne, as it is stated: “Return, Israel, to the Lord your God”
